data.title: Gimme Credit released Indonesia report examining market risk, assessing equity volatility impact.
data.description: On January 31, 2026, financial organization Gimme Credit published a new report focusing on Indonesia, aiming to determine if the recent fluctuations in the equity market may impact the foreign exchange and bond markets. The report examines the potential need for an increased risk premium for credit investors. This analysis comes in the context of ongoing market shifts, with specific attention to the country’s economic stability.

data.title: Johnnie Walker celebrates music weekend with 'Go Go Highball'. Number one Scotch Whisky brand (IWSR 2024). Los Angeles activation with partner Sabrina Carpenter.
data.description: Over the weekend, the Johnnie Walker Black Label 'Go Go Highball' took center stage with a series of star-studded events. The world's leading Scotch Whisky brand, Johnnie Walker, known as the top brand in the world (IWSR 2024), marked the occasion in Los Angeles. The festivities included activations influenced by partner Sabrina Carpenter and celebrated music's impact.

data.title: Two UK-based crypto exchanges linked to Iran's financial system are sanctioned by US Treasury in unprecedented move.
data.description: Two cryptocurrency exchanges based in the UK have been sanctioned by the US Treasury due to their connections with Iran's financial system. This move signifies the first instance of Washington targeting digital asset platforms.

data.title: AG&P and Pragati Infra Solutions to invest $100 million for India's first modular EPC facility in Andhra Pradesh.
data.description: A cutting-edge Heavy Modular Fabrication and Erection Manufacturing Facility is set to be built in Andhra Pradesh, India, with a planned investment totaling USD 100 million. This development was announced during India Energy Week. The facility will significantly enhance the country's industrial capabilities and is spearheaded by AG&P.

data.title: Kevin Warsh faces challenging task as potential head of Federal Reserve. He's known for consensus-building, key as Trump seeks lower rates.
data.description: Mr. Warsh has a reputation for being a consensus builder, a trait that will be crucial as he potentially takes the helm of the Federal Reserve. President Trump has been vocal about his desire for record-low interest rates.

data.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent avoids recommending a hawkish Kevin Warsh for Fed - could create issues.
data.description: Numerous reports indicate that Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent recently refrained from offering President Trump a decisive recommendation regarding the leadership of the central bank. This development comes amidst growing speculation and debate surrounding the selection process for the role. Bessent's decision to withhold a firm suggestion has garnered attention, with many closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds. The intricacies of the situation remain unclear, but this move by Bessent has stirred further curiosity and discussion within political and financial circles.

data.title: Investors turning away from US with falling dollar, stagnant market, and increasing government borrowing.
data.description: There is a growing disapproval from investors towards the United States, evidenced by the weakening of the dollar, the stagnation of the stock market, and an increase in government borrowing rates.

data.title: States can use tools to prevent rising energy costs. Experts suggest ways to lower electricity bills.
data.description: Experts in the energy field have suggested that governors and legislatures possess the means to prevent electricity price hikes and potentially reduce them.

data.title: Top pick for Fed job, Kevin A. Hassett, faced doubts due to close ties to Trump.
data.description: Kevin A. Hassett was initially considered the leading candidate for the Federal Reserve position. However, concerns were raised about his independence due to his strong connections with the president.

data.title: Crypto billionaires invest $40M to combat CA wealth tax & union influence, through Grow California initiative. Chris Larsen & Tim Draper leading.
data.description: Co-founder of Ripple, Chris Larsen, and renowned venture capitalist, Tim Draper, are supporting Grow California, a recent political initiative that opposes wealth tax proposals influenced by unions.

data.title: Stock market tests key levels, gold and silver dive in risk-off shift. Google, AMD, Amazon, Palantir highlight big earnings week. Dow Jones Futures volatile.
data.description: During the past week, the stock market experienced fluctuations around crucial levels, signaling a risk-off sentiment as gold and silver prices declined. Notable companies such as Google, AMD, Eli Lilly, Amazon, and Palantir are set to announce their earnings in the upcoming week, highlighting another significant period for investors. This week, the market dynamics were tested, particularly with the Dow Jones Futures, indicating a possible shift in risk sentiment. The week ahead is anticipated to be eventful with various key players reporting their financial results.

data.title: Dow Jones drops on inflation and Fed news. Gold and silver stocks plunge, while Tesla rallies on SpaceX news.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following unexpected inflation data and news about the Warsh nomination. Conversely, Tesla saw a surge in its stock value due to positive developments with SpaceX. Additionally, the price of gold experienced a significant drop. This event was covered live, highlighting the plunge in gold and silver stocks alongside the Dow's reaction to inflation and Federal Reserve news.

data.title: DraftKings and Flutter Entertainment stocks dropped 8% and 2% on Friday, while a competitor remains strong. Industry's stock market slump continues.
data.description: On Friday, DraftKings and Flutter Entertainment experienced decreases of 8% and 2% in their stock prices. Despite this industry-wide slump, a competitor managed to avoid the same fate. The stock market rally enjoyed by smaller betting companies has reversed, with FanDuel, DraftKings facing price cuts. This information was highlighted by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: SoFi beat Q4 earnings estimates, but stock declined. Analyst defends. Full details at Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Although SoFi reported fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that exceeded Wall Street expectations, the fintech stock unexpectedly reversed direction and trended downward. Even though the company performed well, its stock did not reflect this success. An analyst defended SoFi amidst this reversal in the stock's performance.

data.title: Sandisk stock soars after beating Wall Street targets, with strong results and guidance. Memory chip market booming.
data.description: Sandisk's stock soared following the memory-chip maker's exceptional performance in the December quarter, surpassing Wall Street's expectations. The company's guidance also exceeded projections. The surge in Sandisk's stock reflects the increasing popularity of memory stocks, which are being likened to 'The New Gold'. This news highlights the positive reception of Sandisk's strong financial results and promising future prospects within the investment community.

data.title: Stock market mixed as Meta, Microsoft, Tesla, Sandisk lead huge earnings. Trump makes Fed chief pick.
data.description: In the recent week, the stock market showed a mixed performance driven by significant earnings reports from companies like Tesla, Meta, and Microsoft, along with others. Additionally, there was noteworthy news from the Federal Reserve. Key figures include Tesla, Meta, Microsoft, and Sandisk leading in impressive earnings. Furthermore, former President Trump announced his nomination for the Federal Reserve Chief position. This information was part of a comprehensive weekly review of market activities.

data.title: China's BYD starts trials in Hungary to avoid EU tariffs with a plant that can produce 300,000 cars/year.
data.description: The recently built manufacturing facility by BYD in Szeged, Hungary is set to produce up to 300,000 cars annually. This move by the Chinese electric vehicle company is seen as an effort to bypass European Union tariffs, with trials starting in Hungary.

data.title: Apple faces iPhone shortages due to high demand, record sales in Dec quarter, leading to supply constraints for March quarter.
data.description: During the December quarter, Apple experienced a surge in iPhone sales, reaching record numbers. This high demand quickly depleted the supply, resulting in shortages for the upcoming March quarter. The shortage is attributed to constraints in chip production.

data.title: Regeneron stock dropped, but could pursue deals after strong Q4 report. Analyst hints at potential moves.
data.description: Shares of Regeneron experienced a decline on Friday after the company reported a strong fourth-quarter performance. Despite this drop, an analyst hinted that the company may be inclined to pursue partnerships or acquisitions in the near future. This development signals potential growth opportunities for Regeneron in the wake of its positive quarterly results.

data.title: Approximately $1.82 billion withdrawn from Bitcoin and Ether ETFs as metals surge. Analyst notes BTC outperforming.
data.description: Analyst Eric Balchunas highlighted that Bitcoin significantly outperformed other assets in 2023 and 2024, stating that it had a remarkable performance compared to its counterparts during that period. He noted that the other assets are still lagging behind in catching up to Bitcoin's impressive performance.
